title: IT Support Toolkit
description: A practical toolkit concept for troubleshooting, setup assistance, documentation, and repeated technical support tasks.
summary: A technical support toolkit concept that organizes common checks, setup notes, and support workflows into one usable interface.

sections.heading: Overview
body: This project focuses on the practical side of IT support: repeatable checks, documentation, device setup, troubleshooting, and handoff notes.
The goal is to reduce repeated manual effort and keep support work easier to follow.

sections.heading: Challenge
body: Support work often depends on memory, scattered notes, and manual repetition. That makes it easy to miss steps or lose context.
A useful tool has to stay simple enough to use during real support situations.

sections.heading: Approach
body: I designed the toolkit around categorized checks, quick actions, structured notes, and reusable support references.
The direction can grow into automation scripts, status checks, or internal documentation depending on team needs.

sections.heading: Outcome
body: The outcome is a practical tool direction for small teams that need better support consistency without adopting heavy enterprise software.
It connects my development work with technical IT assistance and troubleshooting.
